mission.title: Install and Configure Kagent on Kubernetes
mission.description: kagent is a Kubernetes native framework for building AI agents, allowing you to easily deploy and manage AI workloads on Kubernetes. Installing kagent enables you to leverage its capabilities for developing intelligent applications in a cloud-native environment.

steps.title: Install kagent via Helm
steps.description:
Add the kagent Helm repository and install:
```bash
helm repo add kagent https://kagent-dev.github.io/kagent
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace kagent
helm install kagent kagent/kagent --namespace kagent --version 0.2.1
```
See https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/introduction/installation for details.

steps.title: Configure an LLM provider
steps.description:
Create a ModelConfig resource to connect to an LLM provider:
```bash
kubectl apply -n kagent -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: kagent.dev/v1alpha1
kind: ModelConfig
metadata:
  name: openai-gpt4
spec:
  provider: openai
  model: gpt-4
  apiKeySecretRef:
    name: openai-key
    key: api-key
EOF
```
Create the secret first:
```bash
kubectl create secret generic openai-key --from-literal=api-key=<your-key> -n kagent
```

steps.title: Create an Agent
steps.description:
Deploy an AI agent:
```bash
kubectl apply -n kagent -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: kagent.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  name: k8s-helper
spec:
  systemPrompt: "You are a Kubernetes expert assistant."
  modelConfigRef:
    name: openai-gpt4
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kagent
kubectl get agents -n kagent
kubectl get modelconfigs -n kagent
```

resolution.summary: kagent is installed and can deploy AI agents on Kubernetes with configurable LLM providers and MCP tools.

uninstall.title: Remove agents and configs
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete agents --all -n kagent
kubectl delete modelconfigs --all -n kagent
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Helm release
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall kagent -n kagent
kubectl delete namespace kagent
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kagent kagent/kagent --namespace kagent
```

troubleshooting.title: Agent not responding
troubleshooting.description:
Check kagent controller logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagent -n kagent
```

troubleshooting.title: LLM API key errors
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the secret exists and has the correct key:
```bash
kubectl get secret openai-key -n kagent -o jsonpath='{.data.api-key}' | base64 -d
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not installed
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep kagent
```
If missing, reinstall the Helm chart with `--set installCRDs=true`.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
